title: ctenitis subconnexa var. alstonii
intro: Ctenitis subconnexa var. alstonii, also known as Alston's ctenitis, is a plant species belonging to the family Polypodiaceae, commonly found in tropical regions of Central and South America. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ctenitis subconnexa var. alstonii include relief from pain, fever reduction, and wound healing, making it a valuable resource in folk medicine. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.
